claim_attributions.claim_text: Deterministic readiness: fatigued (score 49.1).
Aim for roughly 8 to 8.5 hours of sleep nightly this block and protect bedtime before the weekend trail sessions.
Fuel every training day, especially after the long trail run and the hill workout; prioritize carbs and fluids before and after sessions.
Keep strength and mobility light: 10-15 minutes after easy days or on rest days only if it helps recovery.
If resting HR trends upward or legs feel flat, replace any planned quality session with easy aerobic work or full rest.
Recent load is elevated versus baseline and recovery is a little worse than usual, so the plan has to stay conservative early in the block.
HRV data is unavailable, so readiness confidence is lower than ideal and the plan should lean on sleep and resting HR trends.
Trail specificity is limited to weekends by lifestyle constraints, so midweek work stays flat-road or cycling by design.
Re-establish routine after the recent long trail effort while staying recovery-biased.
Maintain aerobic work with low impact so running load stays controlled.
Keep run frequency without adding fatigue.
Absorb the first half of the week and keep fatigue from stacking.
Light neuromuscular touch while staying mostly aerobic.
Build trail durability and climbing economy without a hard session.
Promote recovery while keeping some aerobic flow.
Start the second week with low stress and steady frequency.
Add aerobic volume without impact as the build continues.
Maintain a stable run load while staying below any quality stress.
Reset before the weekend trail session.
Light leg turnover without turning the day into a quality session.
Progress trail durability and climbing tolerance a little while staying controlled.
Absorb the long weekend run and keep movement low impact.
Resume regular running frequency after the weekend trail load.
Continue building aerobic support with minimal musculoskeletal stress.
Maintain run frequency before the weekend trail stimulus.
Create a fresh setup for the weekend hill stimulus.
Light preparation before the first controlled quality day of the block.
Introduce one trail-specific quality stimulus for climbing economy and durability while respecting the fatigue signal.
Absorb the quality day and protect the next running week.
Return to simple aerobic running after the hill stimulus.
Support recovery while keeping overall frequency stable.
Hold a true recovery day in the middle of the week.
Maintain run rhythm while the week stays conservative after the hill day.
Keep some running continuity before the weekend trail session.
Maintain weekend trail specificity while reducing the overall load for consolidation week.
Finish the block with a clean recovery day after the weekend trail session.

data_notes: HRV was unavailable in the last 7 days and baseline window; missing values were treated as empty and not used in averages.
combined_rollups.json was used as the authoritative source for 7d and 28d totals because it is more reliable than manual reconstruction.
Historical peak capacities show the athlete has handled much larger loads before, but those peaks were not used to justify an aggressive near-term ramp because current readiness is fatigued.
The plan starts on 2026-03-23 because 2026-03-22 is the last observed day in the source data and should be treated as 'today'.
No activity/date mismatches were flagged in the provided recent detail.
Readiness status was set from deterministic readiness_and_risk_forecast.json.
Guardrails: enforced ramp rate (max_ramp_pct=0.0%). last7_hours=8.046, allowed_hours=8.046, planned_hours=5.700.

effective_constraints.lifestyle_notes: Only flat road runs or cycling allowed during the week days. Trail runs limited to Saturday or Sunday only.

meta.lifestyle_notes: Only flat road runs or cycling allowed during the week days. Trail runs limited to Saturday or Sunday only.

days.purpose: Re-establish routine after the recent long trail effort while staying recovery-biased.
days.title: Easy road run
days.workout: 10 min easy jog warm-up + dynamic leg swings; 30 min easy continuous road running at conversational effort; 10 min easy jog/walk cool-down + light calf/hip mobility. Keep the pace relaxed and avoid surges. If the legs feel heavy, keep the middle block at the low end of easy.

days.purpose: Maintain aerobic work with low impact so running load stays controlled.
days.title: Easy bike spin
days.workout: 10 min very easy spin warm-up; 35 min easy aerobic ride at smooth cadence; 10 min easy spin cool-down. Keep breathing light and legs fresh. Flat route or trainer preferred.

days.purpose: Keep run frequency without adding fatigue.
days.title: Recovery road run
days.workout: 10 min easy jog warm-up; 25 min easy road running; 10 min cool-down jog/walk. Stay fully conversational and keep the stride compact. No extra tempo or hill work today.

days.purpose: Absorb the first half of the week and keep fatigue from stacking.
days.title: Rest day
days.workout: Rest day. No structured training.

days.purpose: Light neuromuscular touch while staying mostly aerobic.
days.target_intensity: Easy aerobic, RPE 3-4/10, with relaxed strides
days.title: Easy road run + strides
days.workout: 10 min easy jog warm-up; 30 min easy road run; 4 x 20 sec relaxed strides with 60-90 sec easy jog between; 10 min cool-down jog/walk. Keep the strides smooth, not sprinted. If fatigue is still elevated, remove the strides and cap the run at 35-40 min.

days.purpose: Build trail durability and climbing economy without a hard session.
days.target_intensity: Easy aerobic, RPE 3-4/10, hiking on steep climbs
days.title: Long trail aerobic run
days.workout: 15 min easy jog warm-up on the flat; 70 min easy trail run with planned hiking on steep grades; 15 min easy walk/jog cool-down. Keep descents smooth and never force the climbs. Stay strictly aerobic and save any faster work for later blocks.

days.purpose: Promote recovery while keeping some aerobic flow.
days.title: Easy recovery bike
days.workout: 10 min very easy spin warm-up; 20 min easy aerobic ride; 10 min spin cool-down. Keep cadence smooth and effort light after the long trail run. If the legs feel flat, shorten to 25-30 min total.

days.purpose: Start the second week with low stress and steady frequency.
days.title: Easy road run
days.workout: 10 min easy jog warm-up + mobility; 35 min easy road run; 10 min cool-down jog/walk. Keep the effort relaxed and stay flat. No pacing targets other than easy effort.

days.purpose: Add aerobic volume without impact as the build continues.
days.title: Easy bike spin
days.workout: 10 min easy spin warm-up; 35 min easy aerobic ride; 10 min spin cool-down. Keep this truly easy and use it to support recovery and circulation. Flat route or trainer preferred.

days.purpose: Maintain a stable run load while staying below any quality stress.
days.title: Aerobic road run
days.workout: 10 min easy jog warm-up; 30 min steady easy road run; 10 min easy cool-down jog/walk. Keep the effort smooth and even. If the week feels heavy, shorten the middle block by 10 min.

days.purpose: Reset before the weekend trail session.
days.title: Rest day
days.workout: Rest day. No structured training.

days.purpose: Light leg turnover without turning the day into a quality session.
days.target_intensity: Easy aerobic, RPE 3-4/10, with relaxed strides
days.title: Easy road run + strides
days.workout: 10 min easy jog warm-up; 35 min easy road running; 4 x 20 sec relaxed strides with full easy recovery; 10 min cool-down jog/walk. Keep the strides smooth and short. If fatigue lingers, skip the strides and keep the run purely easy.

days.purpose: Progress trail durability and climbing tolerance a little while staying controlled.
days.target_intensity: Easy aerobic, RPE 3-4/10, hiking on climbs
days.title: Long trail aerobic run
days.workout: 15 min easy jog warm-up; 80 min easy trail run with power-hiking on steep climbs; 15 min easy walk/jog cool-down. Keep the effort conversational and protect the descents. This is a build run, not a race simulation.

days.purpose: Absorb the long weekend run and keep movement low impact.
days.title: Easy recovery bike
days.workout: 10 min very easy spin warm-up; 20 min easy aerobic ride; 10 min cool-down. Keep cadence relaxed and stop if the legs feel tired from the trail run. Flat route or stationary bike preferred.

days.purpose: Resume regular running frequency after the weekend trail load.
days.title: Easy road run
days.workout: 10 min easy jog warm-up; 35 min easy road run; 10 min easy jog/walk cool-down. Stay controlled and keep the pace purely conversational. No added quality today.

days.purpose: Continue building aerobic support with minimal musculoskeletal stress.
days.title: Easy bike spin
days.workout: 10 min easy spin warm-up; 40 min easy aerobic ride; 10 min spin cool-down. Keep cadence smooth and the effort light enough to recover from the weekend. Flat route or trainer only.

days.purpose: Maintain run frequency before the weekend trail stimulus.
days.title: Aerobic road run
days.workout: 10 min easy jog warm-up; 30 min steady easy road run; 10 min cool-down jog/walk. Keep the effort light and smooth. If fatigue is present, keep the whole run at the low end of easy.

days.purpose: Create a fresh setup for the weekend hill stimulus.
days.title: Rest day
days.workout: Rest day. No structured training.

days.purpose: Light preparation before the first controlled quality day of the block.
days.target_intensity: Easy aerobic, RPE 3-4/10, with relaxed strides
days.title: Easy road run + strides
days.workout: 10 min easy jog warm-up; 35 min easy road running; 4 x 20 sec relaxed strides with full easy recovery; 10 min cool-down jog/walk. Keep the strides smooth and short. If the week has felt heavy, drop the strides and keep the run easy only.

days.purpose: Introduce one trail-specific quality stimulus for climbing economy and durability while respecting the fatigue signal.
days.title: Controlled trail hills
days.workout: 15 min easy jog warm-up; 4 x 5 min controlled uphill effort with 3 min very easy jog or walk-back recovery; 25 min easy trail cool-down. Keep the reps smooth and repeatable, not maximal. If the week has felt heavy, replace this with 90-100 min easy trail/hike and no reps.

days.purpose: Absorb the quality day and protect the next running week.
days.title: Easy recovery bike
days.workout: 10 min very easy spin warm-up; 30 min easy aerobic ride; 10 min cool-down. Keep cadence smooth and effort light after the hill session. Shorten the ride if the legs feel flat.

days.purpose: Return to simple aerobic running after the hill stimulus.
days.title: Easy road run
days.workout: 10 min easy jog warm-up; 25 min easy road run; 10 min easy jog/walk cool-down. Keep the pace conversational and the surface flat. No extra work today.

days.purpose: Support recovery while keeping overall frequency stable.
days.title: Easy bike spin
days.workout: 10 min easy spin warm-up; 30 min easy aerobic ride; 10 min cool-down. Keep this light and use it for recovery rather than fitness chasing. Flat route or trainer preferred.

days.purpose: Hold a true recovery day in the middle of the week.
days.title: Rest day
days.workout: Rest day. No structured training.

days.purpose: Maintain run rhythm while the week stays conservative after the hill day.
days.title: Easy road run
days.workout: 10 min easy jog warm-up; 20 min easy road run; 10 min easy cool-down jog/walk. Keep the effort relaxed and avoid any surges. This is a refresh run, not a workout.

days.purpose: Keep some running continuity before the weekend trail session.
days.title: Easy road run
days.workout: 10 min easy jog warm-up; 25 min easy road run; 10 min cool-down jog/walk. Stay smooth and conversational. If fatigue is elevated, shorten the middle block to 15-20 min.

days.purpose: Maintain weekend trail specificity while reducing the overall load for consolidation week.
days.target_intensity: Easy aerobic, RPE 3-4/10, hiking on climbs
days.title: Long trail aerobic run
days.workout: 15 min easy jog warm-up; 60 min easy trail run with hiking on steep sections; 15 min easy walk/jog cool-down. Keep the effort strictly aerobic and focus on relaxed climbing mechanics. Do not push the downhills.

days.purpose: Finish the block with a clean recovery day after the weekend trail session.
days.title: Rest day
days.workout: Rest day. No structured training.

actions: Aim for roughly 8 to 8.5 hours of sleep nightly this block and protect bedtime before the weekend trail sessions.
Fuel every training day, especially after the long trail run and the hill workout; prioritize carbs and fluids before and after sessions.
Keep strength and mobility light: 10-15 minutes after easy days or on rest days only if it helps recovery.
If resting HR trends upward or legs feel flat, replace any planned quality session with easy aerobic work or full rest.

risks.message: Recent load is elevated versus baseline and recovery is a little worse than usual, so the plan has to stay conservative early in the block.
HRV data is unavailable, so readiness confidence is lower than ideal and the plan should lean on sleep and resting HR trends.
Trail specificity is limited to weekends by lifestyle constraints, so midweek work stays flat-road or cycling by design.

snapshot.notes: Last 7d load was concentrated in 7 activities with 3 runs, 3 rides, and 1 long trail run; moving time was 8.046h with 1,578m of climbing. Recovery is a bit softer than the 28d baseline: sleep is down and resting HR is slightly up. HRV is unavailable, so the recovery picture is incomplete.
